Integration guide — reusing this sidebar + scaling in another Cursor project
================================================================================

This is a copy/paste workflow for a second project you open in Cursor. You do not
need an npm package or monorepo.


1. What to copy
---------------

  • canvasScale.js (or rename it) — all viewport + scale math.
  • The sidebar UI: the JSX for shell + rail + drawer + backdrop, and the CSS for
    classes such as .sidebar-shell, .sidebar-drawer, .sidebar-rail, .artboard,
    .viewport-fit, etc. In this repo that mostly lives in App.jsx + App.css; you
    can copy the relevant blocks first and split files later.


2. Mount it once in the host app
--------------------------------

  • Use one root element for the scaled layout (like the current artboard div):
    fixed design width and height in px from canvasScale, then
    transform: scale(...) and transformOrigin (e.g. top right) as you have now.
  • On window resize and visualViewport resize, recompute scale the same way and
    set React state (or equivalent).


3. What you customize in the other project
------------------------------------------

  • Inside the scaled area: keep the sidebar and whatever panel content you need.
    Optionally pass children for “main site” vs “drawer content” once you want a
    cleaner split.
  • Outside that tree (optional): the rest of the host page can be normal.
    Avoid applying a second full-page scale on top of the same subtree.


4. Mental model (one line)
--------------------------

  One self-contained “mini page” at a fixed design size (e.g. 2560×1440) that
  shrinks and grows with a single scale factor; the sidebar lives inside that
  mini page.


5. If something looks wrong after paste
---------------------------------------

  • Global CSS in the host (html, body, button, *) may clash. If needed, wrap
    your block in a class (e.g. .luna-root) and scope copied rules under it.
  • rem follows the host root font-size; this design assumes ~16px per rem unless
    you set font-size on your wrapper.


Summary
-------

Copy the scale helper + sidebar markup/styles → one scaled wrapper → same resize
listeners as here. No extra tooling required for a personal second project.
